title: Worker Management
description: Creating, executing, listing, and deleting background workers on the Puter platform via Cloudflare Workers

actors.name: AuthenticatedUser
actors.description: Puter end-user interacting via puter.js SDK (web or app context)

actors.name: PuterApp
actors.description: App context; may own a sandbox app tied to the worker (appId flow)

actors.name: FileSystem
actors.description: Internal system actor – triggers auto-redeploy on fs.write.file events

actors.name: CloudflareWorkers
actors.description: External compute platform; hosts and executes deployed worker scripts

actors.name: ServiceManager
actors.description: putility service orchestrator; registers and initialises WorkerService with dependency resolution

use_cases.name: Create Worker
steps: Authenticate via puter.ui.authenticateWithPuter() if not already authed
Resolve sandbox app (puter.apps.get / puter.apps.create) or look up named app uid
Call driver: utils.make_driver_method → WorkerService.create({filePath, workerName, authorization, appId})
WorkerService: validate name, check subdomain limit (<=100), reserved words, regex
WorkerService: upsert subdomain entity in es:subdomain (workers.puter.<calculatedName>)
WorkerService: read file via LLRead from Puter filesystem (max 10MB)
WorkerService: createWorker(userData, authToken, workerName, preamble+fileData) → Cloudflare API
WorkersHandler: store {filePath, url, deployTime, createTime} in puter.kv 'user-workers'
Return cfData (includes url)
use_cases.preconditions: User authenticated; file exists in Puter FS
use_cases.postconditions: Worker deployed on Cloudflare; subdomain registered; KV updated

use_cases.name: Execute Worker
steps: Authenticate if needed
Construct Request from args
Inject 'puter-auth' header with authToken (unless x-puter-no-auth present)
fetch(req) → worker URL at <workerName>.puter.work
use_cases.postconditions: HTTP response from worker returned to caller

use_cases.name: List Workers
steps: Authenticate if needed
Call driver: WorkerService.getFilePaths({}) with no workerName filter
WorkerService: es:subdomain.select(StartsWith 'workers.puter.')
Map each subdomain entity to {name, url, file_path, file_uid, created_at}
Return array

use_cases.name: Get Worker Info
steps: Authenticate if needed
Normalize workerName to lowercase
Call driver: WorkerService.getFilePaths({workerName})
WorkerService: es:subdomain.select(Eq 'subdomain' = 'workers.puter.<workerName>')
Return first result object

use_cases.name: Delete Worker
steps: Authenticate if needed
Normalize workerName to lowercase
Call driver: WorkerService.destroy({workerName, authorization})
WorkerService: getUserInfo(authorization) to verify ownership
WorkerService: deleteWorker(userData, authorization, workerName) → Cloudflare API
WorkerService: es:subdomain.delete(uid)
WorkersHandler: remove entry from puter.kv 'user-workers'
Return true on success
use_cases.preconditions: Caller must own the worker (uuid check)
use_cases.postconditions: Worker removed from Cloudflare; subdomain entity deleted; KV updated

use_cases.name: Stream Worker Logs
steps: Call driver: WorkerService.getLoggingUrl() → returns config.loggingUrl
Open WebSocket: wss://<loggingEndpoint>/<authToken>/<workerName>
Return logStreamObject (EventTarget + ReadableStream-compatible) on socket open
Emits 'log' MessageEvents; exposes onLog callback and start/cancel for ReadableStream

use_cases.name: Auto-Redeploy Worker on File Change
steps: Event fires when a Puter file is written (same server only)
Check if written file's node is linked to a subdomain starting with 'workers.puter.'
Read updated file content via readPuterFile(actor, filePath)
Resolve auth token: app token (if app_owner) or session token
createWorker(owner, authToken, workerName, preamble+fileData) → Cloudflare
svc_notification.notify(user) with success or failure message
use_cases.notes: Triggered automatically; no direct user action required

use_cases.name: Register & Initialize WorkerService
steps: factory.create({parameters, context}) → WorkerService instance
ServiceManager.maybe_init_: check TService.get_depends() for unresolved deps
If deps ready → ServiceManager.init_service_: call service_impl.init(), status → Running
If deps pending → store in waiting_ map; init triggered after dependency initializes
WorkerService._init() called: sets Cloudflare keys, wires fs.write.file event listener
WorkerService registers 'workers' interface in registry via '__on_driver.register.interfaces'

key_relationships: WorkersHandler (client) depends_on utils.make_driver_method to call WorkerService (backend)
WorkerService uses es:subdomain (entity storage) for subdomain CRUD
WorkerService uses svc_auth for token generation
WorkerService uses svc_su for privilege escalation (sudo)
WorkerService uses svc_notification to notify users of deploy results
WorkerService uses svc_event to listen for fs.write.file auto-redeploy
WorkerService delegates Cloudflare operations to cloudflareDeploy (createWorker, deleteWorker)
ServiceManager orchestrates WorkerService lifecycle with dependency-aware initialization
ServiceManager uses TService interface (putility) to call init() and get_depends()
important_notes: Worker names are normalized to lowercase; only [a-zA-Z0-9_-] allowed
Subdomain format: workers.puter.<calculatedName> mapped to <name>.puter.work URL
A preamble (puter.js bundle) is prepended to all worker scripts before Cloudflare deploy
KV store ('user-workers') is maintained client-side as a local cache of deployed workers
Sandbox app auto-created (sandbox-<workerName>) for user tokens without explicit appName
